title: Harness-level friction injection for high-impact tasks
description: Add a friction prompt layer to the task dispatch pipeline that requires high-impact agents (self-evolve, socialmedia/publish, heavy-tier tasks) to first articulate: (a) what irreversible action they are about to take, (b) what could go wrong, (c) their confidence level — before proceeding. This is not a user-confirmation gate (that was removed 2026-04-07), but an internal agent self-assessment step injected by the harness into the task prompt.
rationale: The reading note identifies that agents strip away the cognitive/social/physical friction humans use as calibration — there is no 'I'm not sure', no sense of decision magnitude. Mira already has two friction points: content guard + preflight for publishing, and audit_skill() for skill imports. But general task dispatch has zero friction: self-evolve, analyst, and heavy-tier tasks are dispatched with the same cost as a lookup. The note asks which layer friction should come from — harness or task structure — and concludes these are not interchangeable with external protocols. Injecting a self-assessment step into the prompt IS harness-level friction that forces the agent to model its own uncertainty before acting, not just follow a rule.
diff_description: In the task dispatch path (core.py or sub_agent.py), define a set HIGH_IMPACT_TASK_TYPES = {'self-evolve', 'socialmedia', 'analyst'} and a FRICTION_PREAMBLE string: 'Before proceeding, state in one sentence: (1) the most irreversible action this task will take, (2) the single biggest risk if you are wrong, (3) your confidence level (high/medium/low) and why. Then proceed.' When dispatching a task whose type or agent is in HIGH_IMPACT_TASK_TYPES, prepend FRICTION_PREAMBLE to the task prompt. No gate, no blocking — just forced articulation. Log the friction response separately so it can be reviewed during weekly reflection. Add a config key FRICTION_ENABLED = True in key config so it can be toggled without a code change.
